  "title": "Procurement Officer",
  "description": "Food Concepts commenced operations in 2001. From inception, our aim has been clear-cut and focused: to revolutionize the food sector in West Africa and to deliver extraordinary satisfaction to our stakeholders.

Since launching in 2004, Chicken Republic (Subsidiary of Food concepts) has already opened 55 stores in Nigeria and Ghana. On this basis, we are proud of the brand&rsquo;s hard-earned reputation as the fastest growing chicken QSR in West Africa and the number 1 chicken QSR in Nigeria (both in revenues and number of outlets). In addition, Chicken Republic was ranked as one of Nigeria&rsquo;s top 20 brands across all categories (Financial Standards Awards 2009).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Ful-time&nbsp;

Job Purpose


	To develop, implement and manage effective, best-in-class systems for the effective management of supplier relationships and interfaces with Food Concepts.


Core Responsibilities and Key Result Areas
Procurement Operations:


	Negotiate with external vendors to secure advantageous terms
	Plan and approve the ordering of necessary goods and services
	Track and report key functional metrics to reduce expenses and improve effectiveness
	Collaborate with key persons to ensure clarity of the specifications and expectations of the company
	Prepare purchasing reports for all the agreements they have with suppliers and vendors
	Prepare plans for the purchase of equipment, services, and supplies.
	Follow and enforce the company&#39;s procurement policies and procedures.
	Manage inventories and maintaining accurate purchase and pricing records.
	Maintain and update supplier information such as qualifications, delivery times, product ranges, etc.
	Maintain good supplier relations and negotiating contracts.
	Research and evaluate prospective suppliers.


Risk and Cost Management:


	Perform risk management for supply contract
	Examine and test existing contracts
	Ensure clear audit trail, process transparency and clarity of roles of individual parties in the Procurement process


Key Performance Indicators


	Quality of business suppliers
	Procurement turnaround time
	Quality of goods/services procured
	Quality of risk management/prevention of risk exposure in the procurement process


Job Specifications


	A good First Degree in Social/Management/Physical Sciences
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in a similar role, especially in the QSR/Food Industry sector
	Membership of the CIPS, CILS, CILCS, CISCOM, Lean Six Sigma or any other related professional qualification is an added advantage


Knowledge Requirements:


	Knowledge of Inventory Management
	Knowledge of Cost management
	Knowledge of applicable legislations, as well as policies and procedures
	Demonstrates expertise in Procurement management

Procurement Officer Job at Food Concepts Plc




        
    

        
        Food Concepts commenced operations in 2001. From inception, our aim has been clear-cut and focused: to revolutionize the food sector in West Africa and to deliver extraordinary satisfaction to our stakeholders.

Since launching in 2004, Chicken Republic (Subsidiary of Food concepts) has already opened 55 stores in Nigeria and Ghana. On this basis, we are proud of the brand’s hard-earned reputation as the fastest growing chicken QSR in West Africa and the number 1 chicken QSR in Nigeria (both in revenues and number of outlets). In addition, Chicken Republic was ranked as one of Nigeria’s top 20 brands across all categories (Financial Standards Awards 2009).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Procurement Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Ful-time 

Job Purpose


	To develop, implement and manage effective, best-in-class systems for the effective management of supplier relationships and interfaces with Food Concepts.


Core Responsibilities and Key Result Areas
Procurement Operations:


	Negotiate with external vendors to secure advantageous terms
	Plan and approve the ordering of necessary goods and services
	Track and report key functional metrics to reduce expenses and improve effectiveness
	Collaborate with key persons to ensure clarity of the specifications and expectations of the company
	Prepare purchasing reports for all the agreements they have with suppliers and vendors
	Prepare plans for the purchase of equipment, services, and supplies.
	Follow and enforce the company's procurement policies and procedures.
	Manage inventories and maintaining accurate purchase and pricing records.
	Maintain and update supplier information such as qualifications, delivery times, product ranges, etc.
	Maintain good supplier relations and negotiating contracts.
	Research and evaluate prospective suppliers.


Risk and Cost Management:


	Perform risk management for supply contract
	Examine and test existing contracts
	Ensure clear audit trail, process transparency and clarity of roles of individual parties in the Procurement process


Key Performance Indicators


	Quality of business suppliers
	Procurement turnaround time
	Quality of goods/services procured
	Quality of risk management/prevention of risk exposure in the procurement process


Job Specifications


	A good First Degree in Social/Management/Physical Sciences
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in a similar role, especially in the QSR/Food Industry sector
	Membership of the CIPS, CILS, CILCS, CISCOM, Lean Six Sigma or any other related professional qualification is an added advantage


Knowledge Requirements:


	Knowledge of Inventory Management
	Knowledge of Cost management
	Knowledge of applicable legislations, as well as policies and procedures
	Demonstrates expertise in Procurement management
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at PATH4AIPOCUS Project
content:
The PATH4AIPOCUS Project is a three-year initiative funded by the Gates Foundation. The project aligns with national and global Maternal, Newborn, Child, and Nutrition Health (MNCNH) priorities by supporting the development, validation, and scale-up of Artificial Intelligence–enabled Point-of-Care Ultrasound (AI-POCUS) within antenatal care (ANC) services in Nigeria.

The project aims to generate robust evidence on health system readiness—including service delivery regulations, policy alignment, competency frameworks, financing mechanisms, and implementation feasibility—to inform sustainable integration of AI-enabled POCUS into routine maternal healthcare in resource-constrained settings.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



1.) State Coordinator

Job Location: Ado-Ekiti, Ekiti
Employment Type: Contract

Description


	The State Coordinator will provide technical and operational oversight for PATH4AIPOCUS implementation at the state level.
	Reporting to the Project Coordinator, the State Coordinator will ensure effective facility-level deployment, stakeholder engagement, quality service delivery, and performance monitoring.
	The role requires strong familiarity with state health systems, maternal health programming, and field-level implementation dynamics.


Responsibilities
State-Level Implementation:


	Coordinate execution of project activities within assigned state(s).
	Lead facility readiness assessments and site activation processes.
	Oversee training, deployment, and operationalization of AI-enabled POCUS devices.


Stakeholder Engagement:


	Serve as the primary liaison with the State Ministry of Health, SPHCDA, facility leadership, Ward Development Committees, and professional bodies.
	Facilitate state-level stakeholder consultations and technical review meetings.
	Support ethical approval processes and regulatory compliance at state level.


Supervision & Quality Assurance:


	Supervise trained providers to ensure adherence to clinical protocols and referral pathways.
	Monitor competency development and provide supportive supervision.
	Ensure implementation meets quality assurance and safety standards.


Monitoring & Reporting:


	Track and report key performance indicators (ANC attendance, referrals, institutional deliveries, device utilization rates).
	Submit monthly and quarterly progress reports.
	Support state-level costing, evaluation, and health systems assessment activities.


Risk Management & Community Engagement:


	Identify and escalate operational risks including security, workforce gaps, and equipment challenges.
	Support culturally appropriate community engagement strategies to enhance uptake.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Medicine, Nursing, Midwifery, Public Health, or related discipline (possession of a postgraduate academic or professional degree is an added advantage).
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in maternal and child health program implementation.
	Experience working with State Ministries of Health and Primary Health Care Development Agencies.
	Strong knowledge of local health systems and socio-cultural contexts.
	Demonstrated skills in reporting, stakeholder engagement, and supervision.
	Proficiency in relevant local language(s) is an added advantage.


Application Closing Date: 1st June, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

2.) Project Monitoring and Evaluation Manager

Job Location: Benin, Edo
Employment Type: Contract

Key Responsibilities
M&E System Design and Leadership:


	Develop and operationalize the project’s M&E framework aligned with project objectives.
	Define indicators, targets, and data collection strategies for:
	Stakeholder engagement and policy alignment
	Facility readiness and provider capacity
	Community demand creation and acceptability
	Training effectiveness and competency
	Service delivery, QA, and implementation outcomes
	Align indicators with global frameworks, including Proctor’s Implementation Outcomes Framework.


Evaluation Design and Execution:


	Lead design and coordination of Baseline, midline, endline, and post-endline assessments
	Oversee mixed-methods evaluation, including: Quantitative surveys, Routine service data analysis, qualitative research (KIIs, FGDs)
	Coordinate closely with external evaluation partners (e.g., Johns Hopkins University) to ensure methodological rigor and alignment.
	Support integration of the AI-POCUS digital monitoring platform with project databases.
	Ensure interoperability with national systems (e.g., DHIS2 where applicable).
	Troubleshoot data system issues in collaboration with IT teams and device vendors.


Quality Assurance and Learning:


	Collaborate with clinical and QA teams to track diagnostic concordance, Provider performance trends, and Fidelity to AI-POCUS protocols
	Translate QA findings into programmatic improvements, training adjustments, and supervision strategies
	Establish feedback loops for continuous learning across facilities and states.


Data Use, Reporting, and Knowledge Translation:


	Lead production of monthly, quarterly, and annual reports, Implementation outcome reports, policy briefs, and dissemination materials
	Train project staff, facility teams, and partners on: M&E tools and frameworks, data collection and reporting standards, and use of dashboards for decision-making
	Build the capacity of the project staff, state, and facility stakeholders to sustain M&E systems beyond the project.


Support for Monitoring, Evaluation, and Research:


	Prepare datasets for: Baseline, midline, endline, and post-endline assessments


Mixed-methods analysis (quantitative + qualitative integration):


	Work with the M&E team to structure data for Proctor’s implementation outcomes framework.
	Support coding frameworks and data export for tools like NVivo (qualitative data).
	Maintain documentation (data dictionaries, metadata, codebooks).


Qualifications and Experience


	Master's Degree in Public Health, Epidemiology, Biostatistics, and Health Economics
	7–10 years of experience in M&E for large-scale health programs.
	Proven experience leading evaluations (baseline to endline) using mixed methods.


Experience in:


	Maternal, newborn, and child health (MNCH)
	Digital health or AI-enabled interventions (preferred)
	Multi-state or multi-country projects
	Experience working with government stakeholders and donors.
	Close attention to detail and data accuracy
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills
	Ability to manage multiple datasets and deadlines.


Application Closing Date: 25th May, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

3.) Project Database Manager

Job Location: Benin, Edo
Employment Type: Contract

Key Responsibilities
Database Design & Development:


	Develop and maintain relational databases for all work packages, including:
	
		Stakeholder mapping and engagement tracking data, Facility readiness and assessment datasets, Community engagement and demand creation tracking Training, certification, and provider competency records., AI-POCUS service delivery, QA, and concordance tracking datasets
		Build data architecture that integrates multiple data sources (HMIS, digital monitoring platforms, surveys, qualitative datasets).
		Design data collection templates compatible with tools such as REDCap, DHIS2, ODK, or CommCare.
	
	


Data Management & Quality Assurance:


	Establish and enforce data management SOPs, including: Data entry protocols Data validation rules, Version control and audit trails.
	Conduct routine data quality assessments (DQAs), including: Completeness Accuracy Consistency across sources.
	Clean and harmonize datasets from multiple streams (facility logs, QA reviews, surveys, KIIs / FGDs).


System Integration & Digital Platform Support:


	Support integration of AI-POCUS digital monitoring platform with project databases.
	Ensure interoperability with national systems (e.g., DHIS2 where applicable).
	Troubleshoot data system issues in collaboration with IT teams and device vendors.


Data Security & Compliance:


	Ensure compliance with national and institutional data protection regulations.


Implement secure data storage, encryption, and controlled access protocols.


	Maintain de-identified datasets for QA review and research purposes.
	Support ethical compliance requirements tied to approvals from national and state ethics committees.


Reporting & Data Visualization:


	Generate routine and ad hoc reports for: Project leadership, State stakeholders Advisory Group.
	Develop dashboards and visualizations for: Implementation outcomes (acceptability, adoption, feasibility, etc.).
	Facility readiness and utilization trends.
	QA and mentorship performance indicators.
	Support preparation of datasets for analysis by internal teams and partners.


Support to Monitoring, Evaluation, and Research:


	Prepare datasets for: Baseline, midline, endline, and post-endline assessments.
	Mixed-methods analysis (quantitative + qualitative integration).
	Work with M&E team to structure data for Proctor’s implementation outcomes framework.
	Support coding frameworks and data export for tools like NVivo (qualitative data).
	Maintain documentation (data dictionaries, metadata, codebooks).


Training & Capacity Building:


	Train field staff, facility staff, and data collectors on: data entry tools, data quality standards and use of dashboards.
	Provide ongoing mentorship and troubleshooting support for data-related issues at facility level.


Qualifications and Experience


	3–5 years of experience in database management for health programs.
	Experience working with large-scale health data systems (e.g., DHIS2, REDCap, ODK).
	Prior experience in maternal and child health, digital health, or implementation research is highly desirable.
	Experience supporting multi-state or multi-site projects preferred.
	Strong proficiency in SQL databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, etc.), data analysis tools (Excel, R, Python, or STATA).
	Experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau).
	Mobile data collection platforms (ODK, CommCare, KoboToolbox).
	Familiarity with Health information systems (e.g., DHIS2), Qualitative data tools (e.g., NVivo).
	High attention to detail and data accuracy.
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
	Ability to manage multiple datasets and deadlines.


Application Closing Date: 25th May, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

 

4.) Implementation Scientist

Job Location: Benin, Edo
Employment Type: Full-time

The Role


	We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Implementation Scientist to provide technical leadership across the implementation research dimensions of the PATH4AI POCUS Project.
	The postholder will be central to designing and executing the project's implementation science strategy, ensuring methodological rigour across study sites, and translating evidence into actionable policy guidance for the Nigerian health system.
	The Implementation Scientist will report directly to the Project Director and will work closely with co-investigators, the project coordinator, state coordinators, the JHU team, the Study Advisory Group, and federal and state government partners.
	This is a substantive research leadership position suited to an individual with a strong track record in implementation science in low- and middle-income country (LMIC) health systems.


Key Responsibilities


	Working in close collaboration with the Project Director, Co-Principal Investigators, and the project coordinators,
	Lead the design and operationalisation of the project's implementation science framework, applying validated frameworks.
	Co-design the study protocol in collaboration with the other members of the team, the Federal Ministry of Health, state governments, device manufacturers, and the Gates Foundation.
	Lead stakeholder mapping, co-design of service delivery and financing models, and facilitation of multi-stakeholder engagement processes at national and state levels.
	Oversee facility and provider readiness assessments, community perception research, and the co-design of demand creation and community engagement strategies across the three project states.
	Provide technical leadership for the design and adaptation of the AI-POCUS training curriculum for non-expert sonographers, and oversight of the expert review panel established for quality assurance and diagnostic concordance tracking.
	Lead the mixed-methods implementation outcomes evaluation at baseline, midline, endline, and post-implementation timepoints, integrating quantitative surveys, service utilisation data, structured observation, and qualitative interviews.
	Oversee referral pathway mapping, care-pathway analysis, and the design of standardised referral tracking systems across implementation and control sites.
	Lead the cross-work package evidence synthesis and contribute to the development of targeted policy briefs for national policymakers, state officials, professional associations, and donor audiences.
	Contribute to peer-reviewed publications and represent the project at national and international conferences and policy forums.
	Prepare high-quality progress reports, technical deliverables, and adaptive management inputs throughout the project lifecycle.


Qualifications and Experience
Essential:


	PhD in implementation science, public health, epidemiology, health systems research, or a closely related field.
	Minimum five years of experience designing and leading implementation research in LMIC settings, with demonstrated expertise in applying PAR principles and validated implementation science frameworks.
	Strong mixed-methods research skills, including quantitative surveys, qualitative IDIs, FGDs, and KIIs, structured observation, and triangulation.
	Experience in maternal, newborn, or reproductive health programmes in Nigeria or sub-Saharan Africa.
	Sound understanding of Nigeria's health system architecture, including PHC structures and relevant regulatory bodies.
	Proven ability to engage government officials, professional associations, and community stakeholders in complex, multi-level environments.
	Proficiency in quantitative analysis software (Stata, R, or SPSS) and qualitative tools (NVivo or Atlas.ti); experience with electronic data capture platforms such as KoboCollect or REDCap.
	Track record of peer-reviewed publications and experience presenting at national or international conferences.
	Excellent written and oral communication skills in English, with the ability to produce high-quality technical and policy documents.
	Willingness and ability to travel regularly to Gombe, Ogun, and Kano States throughout the project period.


Desirable:


	Experience with AI-enabled or digital health implementation in sub-Saharan Africa.
	Familiarity with point-of-care ultrasound, diagnostic imaging, or task-shifting programmes.
	Experience working on Gates Foundation-funded or other major donor-funded research projects.
	Proficiency in Hausa, Yoruba, or other Nigerian languages relevant to the project states.


Application Closing Date: 20th May, 2026.

Click here to apply online

     

    

        Click here to Apply

	Coordinate receiving and dispatch of goods
	Maintain accurate warehouse records and reports

	Must be responsible, proactive, and result-oriented.

Warehouse Manager Job at Adenal Consulting




        
    

        
        Adenal Consulting - Our client, a reputable trading company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Manager

Job Location: Lagos Mainland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	A reputable trading company is seeking a smart, experienced, and detail-oriented Warehouse Manager with a background in Chemistry to join our team.


Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee daily warehouse operations
	Monitor inventory and stock movement
	Ensure proper storage and safety compliance for chemical products
	Coordinate receiving and dispatch of goods
	Maintain accurate warehouse records and reports
	Supervise warehouse staff and ensure operational efficiency


Requirements


	B.Sc./HND in Chemistry, Biochemistry or related fields
	Minimum of 7 years of proven experience in warehouse/inventory management
	Good knowledge of chemical handling and storage procedures
	Strong organizational and leadership skills
	Ability to manage stock records and warehouse operations efficiently
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and inventory systems is an added advantage
	Must be responsible, proactive, and result-oriented.


Method of Application

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Fleet Officer",
  "description": "At Coollink, our passion drives us to create innovative technology solutions that empower enterprises and SMEs across Nigeria and Africa. For over two decades, we&rsquo;ve been pioneering satellite broadband and IT services, bringing reliable, affordable connectivity to even the most remote regions. Now, we&rsquo;re expanding our expertise to deliver more than just connectivity&mdash;we provide end-to-end IT solutions that fuel businesses of all sizes.

From startups and SMEs to large enterprises, we offer the tools and support necessary to thrive in an increasingly digital world. Our IT services cover everything from cloud solutions and network security to managed services, ensuring businesses stay connected, secure, and efficient. With our Partners, we&rsquo;re pushing the boundaries of what&#39;s possible, ensuring businesses and communities across Africa have access to world-class connectivity and IT solutions. Whether it&#39;s urban hubs or remote locations, geography is no barrier&mdash;we connect people to the technology that transforms lives and industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Officer
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a Fleet Officer to manage the company&rsquo;s vehicles and support daily fleet operations.
	The ideal candidate should be organized, smart, detail-oriented, and good with Microsoft Office tools, Excel and Outlook.
	The role also requires strong documentation, reporting, and coordination skills to ensure smooth fleet operations.


Responsibilities


	Monitor daily vehicle and fleet activities.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly fleet reports.
	Ensure all fleet documents are properly filled, filed and uploaded.
	Keep records of vehicle maintenance, insurance, licenses, and other fleet-related documents.
	Track vehicle servicing, repairs, and fuel usage.
	Coordinate vehicle maintenance and follow up when necessary.
	Work with drivers, vendors, and service providers to ensure smooth operations.
	Support general administrative and operational tasks related to the fleet.


Requirements


	HND/Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in a related field.
	2&ndash;5 years of experience in fleet, logistics, or operations support.
	Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel and Outlook.
	Good documentation and reporting skills.
	Smart, organized, and able to pay attention to details.
	Good communication and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to manage tasks effectively and work under pressure.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: career@coollink.ng using &quot;Fleet Officer&quot; as the subject of the email.",

  },

}



Fleet Officer Job at Coollink




        
    

        
        At Coollink, our passion drives us to create innovative technology solutions that empower enterprises and SMEs across Nigeria and Africa. For over two decades, we’ve been pioneering satellite broadband and IT services, bringing reliable, affordable connectivity to even the most remote regions. Now, we’re expanding our expertise to deliver more than just connectivity—we provide end-to-end IT solutions that fuel businesses of all sizes.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



From startups and SMEs to large enterprises, we offer the tools and support necessary to thrive in an increasingly digital world. Our IT services cover everything from cloud solutions and network security to managed services, ensuring businesses stay connected, secure, and efficient. With our Partners, we’re pushing the boundaries of what's possible, ensuring businesses and communities across Africa have access to world-class connectivity and IT solutions. Whether it's urban hubs or remote locations, geography is no barrier—we connect people to the technology that transforms lives and industries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Officer
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a Fleet Officer to manage the company’s vehicles and support daily fleet operations.
	The ideal candidate should be organized, smart, detail-oriented, and good with Microsoft Office tools, Excel and Outlook.
	The role also requires strong documentation, reporting, and coordination skills to ensure smooth fleet operations.


Responsibilities


	Monitor daily vehicle and fleet activities.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly fleet reports.
	Ensure all fleet documents are properly filled, filed and uploaded.
	Keep records of vehicle maintenance, insurance, licenses, and other fleet-related documents.
	Track vehicle servicing, repairs, and fuel usage.
	Coordinate vehicle maintenance and follow up when necessary.
	Work with drivers, vendors, and service providers to ensure smooth operations.
	Support general administrative and operational tasks related to the fleet.


Requirements


	HND/Bachelor’s Degree in a related field.
	2–5 years of experience in fleet, logistics, or operations support.
	Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel and Outlook.
	Good documentation and reporting skills.
	Smart, organized, and able to pay attention to details.
	Good communication and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to manage tasks effectively and work under pressure.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using "Fleet Officer" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
